steelman_no_trade.draft: A rational Senior could pass because rNPV outcomes are wide, clinical and launch probabilities are uncertain, and the public filings already show negative operating income after the COVID revenue reset.
steelman_no_trade.source_label: C-4 rNPV scenarios and MRNA filed spine
steelman_no_trade.excerpt_or_summary: The filed scenario set and spine identify a wide optionality range and current operating losses.

counterparty.draft: The plausible counterparty is a risk-budgeted biotech holder or index participant selling because near-term losses, launch uncertainty, and trial risk may not fit their mandate even if pipeline option value remains.
counterparty.source_label: C-1 Business and C-4 rNPV scenarios
counterparty.excerpt_or_summary: Business and scenario artifacts identify clinical, regulatory, and launch uncertainty as the central disagreement mechanism.

draft.mechanism: Generalist holders can underweight a portfolio of clinical options after the COVID revenue reset because losses screen poorly before binary program updates.
draft.persistence_reason: The disagreement can persist until multiple readouts, approvals, or commercial launches convert pipeline optionality into observable revenue.

structural_mispricing.source_label: C-2 Moat and C-4 rNPV scenarios
structural_mispricing.excerpt_or_summary: Moat and scenario drafts support platform optionality and route-specific program drivers.

variant_view.draft: The variant view is that the market is over-indexing on COVID revenue decline and underweighting rNPV from respiratory launches and oncology or rare-disease pipeline milestones.
variant_view.source_label: C-1 Business, C-2 Moat, and C-4 rNPV scenarios
variant_view.excerpt_or_summary: Filed Analyst artifacts support pipeline optionality rather than a DCF cash-generator frame.

draft.event: Regulatory decisions or pivotal clinical updates for non-COVID pipeline programs
draft.timing: next six to eight reported quarters
draft.why_it_matters: Converts or breaks rNPV probability assumptions.

draft.event: Seasonal respiratory vaccine demand and product revenue disclosures
draft.why_it_matters: Tests whether the commercial base can support pipeline funding.

catalysts.source_label: C-4 rNPV scenarios and C-6 risk setup
catalysts.excerpt_or_summary: Scenario and risk artifacts identify program probability and launch demand as thesis drivers.

cruxes.claim: Pipeline probability must improve enough to offset cash burn.
cruxes.threshold_value: at least one material non-COVID program advances or receives approval without a major safety setback

cruxes.claim: Respiratory franchise revenue must stabilize the funding base.
cruxes.threshold_value: seasonal revenue decline slows or stabilizes versus the filed bear path

cruxes.claim: Cash runway must remain sufficient for value-creating readouts.
cruxes.threshold_value: cash resources fund the next two years of priority readouts without punitive dilution

source_evidence_summary.business: Business artifact frames MRNA as an mRNA platform and vaccine/pipeline optionality case.
source_evidence_summary.moat: Moat artifact supports platform learning while flagging lack of mature cash-generator economics.
source_evidence_summary.capalloc: Capital allocation artifact supplies cash-runway and reinvestment context.
source_evidence_summary.scenarios: Scenario artifact identifies rNPV-specific program, launch, and runway drivers.
source_evidence_summary.mechanical: Gate card, method directive, and spine provide mechanical context without DCF artifacts.
